content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Comrnission
Septenber 5, 1990
,faDes P . Hccornack
510-260 Stone Road
Susanville, cA 96130
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our file No. I-9 0-4 4 7
Dear Mr. llccormack:
this is in response to your request for advice regarding your
responsibilities under the conflic!-of-interest 
provisions of the
political 
Reform Act (the rraggrrl.l/ Since your reguest is in the
nature of general assistance, we consider your letter to be a
request for infoqrqal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329 (c)
(copy enclosed).2/
OUESTION
The volunteer fire chief for two community services districts
wishes to seII comnunications eguipment to the same districts for
which he serves as fire chief. 
Does this situation create a
conflict-of-interest 
under the Act?
CONCLUSION
There is no prohibition under the Political 
Reform Act
preventing a public official 
from appearing in his private
capacity to represent his interests before an agency under the
appointive or budgetary control of his own agency. Hos/ever, the
official 
may not participate in his or her official 
capacity in a
governmental decision on the same matter. AIso, issues may arise
under Section 1090 of the Government code.
7/ Government code sections 81ooo-91015. AlL statutory
references are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regnrlations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18o0o, et seq. All references to requlations are to Title
2, Division 5 of the California Code of Regmlations.
2/ fnfornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
innunity provided by an opinion or fo!'nal written advice.
(Section 83113; Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo o P.o. Box 8oz . sacramento cA 95804.0802 a (916) 3 z2.s66o


Our FLle No. I-9O-447
Page 2
FACTS
The volunteer fire chief for the Eagle Lake conmunity
Services District and the Stones-Bengard Community Services
District is also the sole proprietor of l.llccormack Electronics
service which sells and services thlo-sray radio conmunications
equipnent. He rrishes to seII communications equipment to these
sane two districts 
and wants to know if this is pernissible under
the Act.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official 
from naking,
participating in, or using his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knorvs or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. 
An official 
has a financial interest in
a decision within the neaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official 
or a nember of his or her imnediate faroily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investnent worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or Dore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or nore.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terns available to the public without regard to
officiat status, aggregating two hundred fifty
doLlars ($250) or nore in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 nonths prior to the tine when the
decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
officiat 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of nanagenent.
(e) ltny donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars (9250) or nore in value
provided to, received by, or prourised to the public
official 
within 12 nonths prior to the tirne when
the decision is nade.
Section 87103, (copy enclosed).
Therefore, an official 
is disqualified from participating in
a decision that will have a foreseeable and uraterial financial
effect on any of his economic interests. 
A public official
includes all neurbers, officers, enployees or consultants of a


our File No. r-9 o-4 4 7
Page 3
state or local government agency. (Section a2o48. ) Section 82041
defines trlocal government agencyrr as a county, city or district 
of
any kind including rrany department, bureau, office, board
coumission or other agency of the foregoing.ri As the volunteer
fire chief for the Eagle Lake community Services District and the
stones-Bengard community Services District, 
it would appear that
you are a public official 
of a Ioca1 government aqency.
As a public official, 
you would be disqualified fron
participating in discussions, voting on a natter, or otherwise
trying to influence members of your own agency concernj.ng a
decision in which you have a financial interest. 
Prohibited
Itatternpts to influence a decision" include contacting any nember,
officer, 
eroployee of the officiat's 
agency or any agency under the
appointive or budqetary control of the official's 
agency.
(ReguJ.ation 18700.1(a), copy enclosed. )
A disqualified official 
also cannot represent or purport to
represent his agency before another aqency regarding a.decision in
which the official 
has a financial interest. 
(Regulation
18700.1(c), copy enclosed.) This inctudes using official
stationery to communicate with a nenber, officer, 
employee. or
consultanL of another agency. However, a disqualified official
can appear before his agency or another agency in his private
capacity to represent his interests. 
(Regulation 18700.1(b) (1).)
It would appear fron the lirnited facts provided to us in your
letter that you are interested in selling communications equipnent
to the sane districts for which you serve as a volunteer fire
chief. 
This being the case, there is no prohibition under the
PoliticaL Reforn Act preventing you frorn appearing in your private
capacity to represent your interests before an altency under the
appointive or budgetary control of the your orrrn agency.
However, other provisions of the 1aw, such as Section 1090 of
the Government code,-may have some bearing on your situation.3/
3/ Governnent code Section 1o9o states:
Members of the Legislature, state, county,
district, judicial district, and city officers or
employees shall not be financially interested in
any contract made by them in their official
capacity, or by any body or board of which they are
members. Nor sha11 state, county, district,
judicial district, and city officers or ernployees
be purchasers at any sale or vendors at any
purchase nade by then in their official capacity.
As used in this article, rrd.istrictrr neans
agency of the state formed pursuant to general law
or special act, for the local performance of
governmental or proprietary functions within
lirnited boundaries.


Our File No. I-90-447
Page 4
The conmission has no jurisdiction to interpret or enforce Section
1o90. The commission's jurisdiction is liroited to the Political
Reform Act. Questions concerning section 1090 should be addressed
to the Attorney ceneral's office or your County Counsel .
I hope this letter provides you with enough guidance. I also
have enclosed a copy of a panphlet concerning conflicts of
interest. 
If you have questions about your participating in a
specific pending decision, you may request additional advice.
Please call me at (915) 322-590L if you have any questions about
this letter.
Sincere Iy,
Scott HalLabrin
Acting GeneraL counsel
滋

By: Deanne Stone
qounsel, Legal Division
SH: DS : aa
Enclosures
